ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-10-2018 Granted Per Written Order

Off Calendar Nov-21-2018 Continued To Dec-10-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-07-2018 Continued To Nov-21-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-10-2018 Continued To Nov-07-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jonathan Huber appeared by telephone for Peter Garcia.

The Court noted that the supplement was not verified or signed by the Administrator. Mr. Huber stated that he filed a declaration rather than a supplement, that his Client is elderly and lives in Nevada, and that he is having a very difficult time communicating with him. The Court stated that Probate rules require a Fiduciary to verify these things, that it is the Fiduciary's responsibility to make sure what the facts are, that the inventory shows $150,000.00 was received and that it is now being told there is nothing in the Estate. Mr. Huber stated that there was litigation that settled, that the Court approved the settlement agreement which indicated that the were no assets and that in January he received a note from the Probate Examiner telling him that the inventory and appraisal needed to be filed. Mr. Huber also stated that at this point he will have to file a motion to be relieved as Counsel, that he has done everything he can, that the Court is not allowing them to close the Estate and that he did an out of State verification. The Court informed Counsel that he needs his Client to take responsibility of these facts and verify the inventory and appraisal, that the motion to be relieved may or may not be approved, and that his Client is subject to all of the rules of a Fiduciary regardless of where he lives. The First and Final Report of Administrator, etc. is continued to November 7, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Mr. Huber stated that he has prepared the petition, is waiting to get the client's signature, the client lives in Nevada, and is currently out of the country. The Petition to Close Estate without Further Administration is continued to May 21, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Lynn O'Malley Taylor. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Off Calendar Jul-12-2016 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jul-06-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Huber (by CourtCall) for petitioner Peter Garcia; Konstantine Demiris (by CourtCall) for objector Marv Claudio.

The Court asked counsel to address provision of notice to the decedent's daughter, and Mr. Claudio's standing to raise objections to the petition for probate. Mr. Huber stated that the petitioner made exhaustive efforts to locate Delphine Kirk, the adult daughter of the decedent, even seeking her whereabouts through contacting her father, with no avail. Mr. Demiris stated that Mr. Claudio has no additional information to provide regarding where his daughter can be found. He asserted that under the provisions of subdivision (a) of Probate Code section 48, a creditor of an estate is an interested party in the action. He stated that Mr. Claudio has a one-half interest in the real property and paid all expenses, so that at least to the estate's share of the expenses he is a creditor of the estate and an interested party. He does not dispute that the estate has an interest in the property sale proceeds. Mr. Huber responded that the estate's interest in the property is separate from that of Mr. Claudio's, and accordingly does not provide a basis for Mr. Claudio's claim to be a creditor of the estate. The Court took the matter under submission, and set a further hearing date of July 12, 2016 at 9:00 am in Department 204 in the event the Court desired additional argument of the matter.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy John Boncato. (Not reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar May-11-2016 Continued To Jun-14-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-30-2016 Continued To May-11-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Huber for (petitioner) Peter Garcia. Konstantine Demaris (via court call) for (interested party) Marv Claudio.

The Court stated that we are here on Mr. Garcia's petition for letters of administration and asked if the decedent was living in San Francisco at the time of death. Mr. Huber stated that he was. The Court stated that there is a procedural issue regarding the notice of hearing to Delphine. Mr. Huber stated that they searched for her address, that they had not received the notice back, that they contacted relatives through Facebook and was told that they had not been in contact with her for three years. Mr. Demaris stated that Delphine's father, Michael Moore should have been contacted, that Delphine lives with him and that they just found this out last night. The Court asked if there will be an objection from Marv Claudio. Mr. Demaris stated that they are working through it, that they have some concerns and that they would like to talk to Mr. Huber. The Court asked if it would be on his client claiming precedence. Mr. Demaris stated that there could be a conflict of interest. The Court stated that the fact that someone with interest and priority to serve is routine, that it wants objections, if made, to be on a valid basis, that the matter needs to be continued for notice to Delphine and that if there is an objection it must be in writing and filed one week prior to the continuance date. The petition for letters of administration was continued to May 11, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and objections are to be filed no later than May 4, 2016.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
